unit.note: All THREE test roots CI runs were executed locally (shared/tests, shared/scripts/tests, shared/scripts/tools/tests): 4456 + 197 + 248 passed. An earlier run covered only shared/tests and missed a wording assertion in shared/scripts/tools/tests/test_record_event.py, which CI caught - the local run now mirrors the CI loop. test_events_log.py::test_latest_event_dt_skips_corrupt_lines and ::test_latest_event_dt_all_corrupt_returns_none pass UNCHANGED: they pin skip-on-WHOLLY-undecodable, which this change preserves. The behaviour change is strictly additive.

integration.note: shared/tests/test_events_newline_integrity.py - end-to-end through the REAL record_event writers and the REAL config.read_events reader, plus the adopt plugin's lock-free event_seeder loaded from its own path.

e2e.note: No UI or HTTP surface: this is a Python library/CLI boundary. The equivalent end-to-end proof is the integration layer above, which drives the real writers and reader rather than a re-implementation.

smoke.note: record_event append + config.read_events round-trip exercised directly by the integrity suite.

surface_verification.justification: The changed surface is a Python append/read boundary with no UI and no HTTP endpoint. It is driven through its PUBLIC API - tools.record_event.append_event and lib.config.read_events - against real temp-dir event logs, including the concatenated-line shape an ordinary git union merge produces. The adopt plugin's second, lock-free writer is loaded from its own file path so the ADR-045 import barrier is exercised as it really is, not stubbed.

behaviors.behavior: append_event onto an unterminated log does not concatenate
append onto an already-terminated log injects no blank line
append onto a missing or zero-byte log injects no leading newline
read_events recovers BOTH records from a concatenated line
read_events recovers partially - valid record kept, remainder reported
recovered records keep wire order across lines (last-wins projections depend on it)
a wholly undecodable line is still skipped, not fatal (no regression)
read_events releases the file handle (explicit context manager)
behaviors.evidence: ::test_read_events_releases_the_file_handle PASSED - recorded honestly as a hygiene guard, it passed pre-fix too

behaviors.behavior: the lock-free adopt event_seeder also terminates before appending
behaviors.evidence: ::test_adoption_seeder_does_not_concatenate_onto_an_unterminated_log PASSED, loading the plugin module by path

behaviors.behavior: append_event_idempotent probes only AFTER dedup, so a skipped duplicate never touches the file
behaviors.evidence: covered by shared/tests/test_record_event_lifecycle_integrity.py dedup cases, re-run green in the full suite

degraded.detail: LOCAL-ONLY failure, invisible to CI: shared/tests/test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact.py fails on an UNRELATED, FOREIGN decision drop (iterate-2026-07-18-fr-existence-gate, impact=convention) that has no conventions.md entry. That drop is UNTRACKED and lives in the developer main tree gitignored decision-drops dir, which a fresh CI clone does not have - so CI passes this test. It belongs to another unfinished iterate: not caused by, and not fixable within, this run. Verified: after adding THIS run architecture.md entry, this run_id no longer appears in the failure list; only the foreign drop remains.
